reasoning: `ClusterPt x (𝓟 Uᶜ)` means every neighborhood of x meets Uᶜ, which is precisely "x is a limit of (i.e., is in the closure of) Uᶜ". Since x ∈ U implies x ∉ Uᶜ, the distinction between `ClusterPt` and `AccPt` is immaterial here, so the Lean version matches "x is a limit point of Uᶜ" regardless of which convention "limit" uses. The quantifier ∀ x ∈ U correctly renders "none of its points", and the outer iff matches "open iff ...". Generalizing from Pugh's metric-space setting to an arbitrary `TopologicalSpace` is appropriate because the filter-based cluster-point formulation is the standard topological analogue and the biconditional holds in any topological space. Thus the Lean signature is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
